title: limonium gmelini subsp. tomentellum
intro: Limonium gmelini subsp. tomentellum, also known as a type of sea lavender, is a flowering plant belonging to the plumbaginaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of using limonium gmelini subsp. tomentellum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
